skill_areas.name: Dribbling & Ball Carrying
skill_areas.description: Close ball control, change of direction, and beating defenders 1v1 using skill moves. The foundation of individual attacking play.
pain_points: Ball getting too far ahead when dribbling at speed
Cannot use weak foot while dribbling
Losing the ball in tight spaces
Lack of confidence to take on defenders
Head always down when dribbling
drills: Cone weaving with both feet (inside-outside)
Inside-outside touch drill at speed
1v1 dribbling challenge with defender in a grid
Maradona turn and step-over practice
Ball mastery circuit: rolls, drags, Cruyff, elastico

skill_moves.name: Step Over
skill_moves.description: Circle foot over the ball to fake direction change. Simple but effective at all levels.

skill_moves.name: Cruyff Turn
skill_moves.description: Fake a pass/shot then drag ball behind standing leg. Iconic move invented by Johan Cruyff.

skill_moves.name: Body Feint
skill_moves.description: Drop shoulder one way, push ball the other. Requires timing and conviction.

skill_moves.name: Ronaldo Chop
skill_moves.description: Jump and chop ball behind standing leg at pace. Best used when running at full speed down the wing.

skill_moves.name: Elastico (Flip Flap)
skill_moves.description: Push ball outside then snap inside in one fluid motion. Ronaldinho's signature move.

skill_moves.name: Roulette (Maradona/Zidane Turn)
skill_moves.description: 360-degree spin shielding ball from defender. Elegant and effective in tight spaces.

skill_moves.name: Neymar Rainbow Flick
skill_moves.description: Flick ball over opponent with heels. High-risk, high-reward showpiece move.

skill_moves.name: La Croqueta
skill_moves.description: Quick lateral shift ball from one foot to the other. Iniesta's signature move for evading tackles.

progression_path: Master basic ball control with both feet
Learn step-over and body feint
Practice Cruyff turn and drag-back
Combine moves in 1v1 situations
Add advanced moves: elastico, roulette
Apply moves at game speed in matches
common_mistakes: Attempting skill moves too far from the defender
Not selling the fake with body movement
Always cutting to the same side
Doing moves for show rather than to create space
Not accelerating after beating the defender

skill_areas.name: Passing & Distribution
skill_areas.description: Short passes, long balls, through balls, crosses, and first-touch passing for build-up play. The most used skill in football.
pain_points: Inaccurate short passes under pressure
Cannot weight long balls correctly
Poor first touch killing passing options
Not looking up before passing
Crosses rarely find a teammate
One-touch passing breaks down in fast play
drills: Two-touch passing triangle drill
Long ball accuracy to target zones (5 yard squares)
Wall pass (give and go) drill in pairs
Rondo (keep-away) games: 4v1, 5v2, 4v4+3
Crossing from wide areas to near/far post targets
One-touch passing in a circle with movement

pass_types.name: Short Pass (Inside Foot)
pass_types.description: The bread-and-butter pass. Side-foot for accuracy. Lock ankle, follow through to target.

pass_types.name: Long Pass (Lofted)
pass_types.description: Hit under the ball with instep. Used to switch play or find runners behind the defence.

pass_types.name: Through Ball
pass_types.description: Weighted pass into space ahead of a runner. Timing and vision are everything.

pass_types.name: One-Touch Pass
pass_types.description: Pass without controlling first. Speeds up play and catches opponents off guard.

pass_types.name: Crossing (Low/High)
pass_types.description: Delivery from wide areas into the box. Low driven crosses or lofted deliveries.

pass_types.name: Chipped Pass
pass_types.description: Delicate lofted pass over short distance to beat pressing opponents.

pass_types.name: Outside Foot Pass
pass_types.description: Disguised pass using outside of foot. Hard to read for defenders.

progression_path: Master inside-foot short passing accuracy
Develop both-foot passing ability
Learn long ball technique and weight
Practice through balls with timing
Add crossing from wide positions
Master one-touch and outside-foot passes
common_mistakes: Not opening body to see the full pitch before receiving
Passing to the player instead of into their path
Toe-poking instead of using the inside of the foot
Not following through toward the target
Telegraphing the pass by staring at the target

skill_areas.name: Shooting & Finishing
skill_areas.description: Finishing techniques including power shots, placement, volleys, headers, and composure in front of goal. Goals win games.
pain_points: Shooting over the bar consistently
No power on weak foot shots
Poor composure in 1v1 with goalkeeper
Cannot finish on the volley
Snatching at chances under pressure
Leaning back too much when shooting
drills: Shooting from the edge of the box: placement vs power
1v1 finishing against goalkeeper
Volley and half-volley finishing drill (served crosses)
Weak foot shooting: 50 reps daily
First-time finish from cutbacks
Header finishing from crosses

shot_types.name: Power Shot (Laces)
shot_types.description: Strike through the center with laces. Plant foot next to ball, follow through.

shot_types.name: Finesse Shot (Curled)
shot_types.description: Side-foot curl into far corner. Used from angles outside the box.

shot_types.name: Chip Shot
shot_types.description: Delicate lob over advancing goalkeeper. Requires extreme composure.

shot_types.name: Volley
shot_types.description: Strike ball out of the air. Keep knee over ball, lock ankle.

shot_types.name: Bicycle Kick (Overhead)
shot_types.description: Acrobatic overhead kick. Spectacular but risky. Only attempt in safe situations.

shot_types.name: Header
shot_types.description: Direct the ball with forehead. Power from neck and core, not head movement.

shot_types.name: Placed Finish (Side Foot)
shot_types.description: Calm side-foot finish into corners. Accuracy over power in close range.

progression_path: Master the basic laces power shot
Learn placed finishing with side foot
Develop finesse curl shots
Practice 1v1 composure against keepers
Add volleys and headers
Master weak foot finishing
common_mistakes: Leaning back causing the ball to fly over the bar
Not planting the standing foot next to the ball
Looking up at the goal instead of at the ball at moment of contact
Using too much power in close-range situations
Not following up shots for rebounds

skill_areas.name: Defending & Tackling
skill_areas.description: Tackling, positioning, marking, pressing, and 1v1 defensive technique. Good defence wins championships.
pain_points: Diving in and getting beaten
Poor body position when jockeying
Losing aerial duels
Switching off on set pieces
Getting turned by attackers
Not communicating with defensive partners
drills: 1v1 jockeying and channeling drill (force to weak foot)
Slide tackle technique on safe surface
Heading clearance from crosses
Defensive line movement and offside trap drill
Recovery run and tracking drill
Pressing triggers: 2v1 pressing exercises

defensive_skills.name: Jockeying
defensive_skills.description: Stay on feet, stay side-on, delay attacker. Force them where you want them to go.

defensive_skills.name: Standing Tackle
defensive_skills.description: Time the tackle to win the ball cleanly. Get body weight behind it.

defensive_skills.name: Slide Tackle
defensive_skills.description: Last resort tackle. Commit fully, win the ball, stay legal.

defensive_skills.name: Aerial Duel
defensive_skills.description: Timing, positioning, and courage. Attack the ball at the highest point.

defensive_skills.name: Pressing
defensive_skills.description: Coordinated high press to win ball back. Read pressing triggers.

defensive_skills.name: Covering & Tracking
defensive_skills.description: Cover passing lanes and track runners making off-the-ball movements.

defensive_skills.name: Ball-Playing from Back
defensive_skills.description: Playing out from defence under pressure. Modern defenders must be comfortable on the ball.

progression_path: Learn body shape for 1v1 defending
Master jockeying and delaying
Practice standing tackles
Develop aerial ability
Understand pressing triggers and team pressing
Learn to play out from the back under pressure
common_mistakes: Diving in instead of staying on feet
Being flat-footed instead of on toes
Watching the ball instead of the attacker's hips
Not communicating with teammates
Ball watching instead of tracking runners

skill_areas.name: Goalkeeping
skill_areas.description: Shot stopping, positioning, distribution, crosses, 1v1 saving, and sweeper keeper play. The most specialized position.
pain_points: Wrong positioning for shots from angles
Fear of diving to the ground
Poor distribution with feet
Misjudging crosses and coming off the line
Getting beaten at the near post
Weak communication with defence
drills: Rapid-fire shot stopping: 10 shots in 30 seconds
Cross claiming under pressure with outfield players
1v1 rushing: when to come out, how to spread
Goal kick and distribution accuracy to zones
Diving technique: collapse, extension, high
Footwork: shuffling, set position, recovery

goalkeeper_skills.name: Set Position
goalkeeper_skills.description: Ready stance: feet shoulder-width, knees bent, weight forward, hands ready.

goalkeeper_skills.name: Shot Stopping (Low)
goalkeeper_skills.description: Collapse dive to the side. Get behind the ball. Smother technique.

goalkeeper_skills.name: Shot Stopping (High)
goalkeeper_skills.description: Extension dive reaching across goal. One-handed saves at full stretch.

goalkeeper_skills.name: Cross Claiming
goalkeeper_skills.description: Come off line to punch or catch crosses. Command the box.

goalkeeper_skills.name: 1v1 Saving
goalkeeper_skills.description: Rush out, make yourself big, stay on feet as long as possible. Star shape.

goalkeeper_skills.name: Distribution (Feet)
goalkeeper_skills.description: Goal kicks, short passes, long balls. Modern keepers must play like outfield players.

goalkeeper_skills.name: Sweeper Keeper
goalkeeper_skills.description: High starting position to intercept through balls. Requires reading the game and pace.

goalkeeper_skills.name: Penalty Saving
goalkeeper_skills.description: Study the taker, pick a side, commit fully. Psychological battle.

progression_path: Master set position and basic handling
Learn collapse and extension dives
Develop distribution with both feet
Practice cross claiming and box command
Train 1v1 scenarios
Add sweeper keeper play and playing from the back
common_mistakes: Standing too upright in set position
Falling backwards instead of diving forward
Coming for crosses you cannot reach
Not communicating with defenders
Parrying into dangerous areas instead of away
Kicking distribution always long instead of building from the back

skill_areas.name: Football Fitness & Conditioning
skill_areas.description: Endurance, speed, agility, strength, and match-specific conditioning for 90-minute performance. Football demands repeated sprints, changes of direction, and sustained effort.
pain_points: Fading in the second half
Slow recovery between sprints
Lack of explosive acceleration
Injury from poor warm-up or overtraining
Poor agility and change of direction speed
Not strong enough in physical duels
drills: Interval running: 30s sprint, 30s jog x 10
Agility ladder: in-in-out-out, crossover, Icky shuffle
Shuttle runs with ball (5m-10m-15m-20m)
Yo-Yo intermittent recovery test (benchmark)
Resistance band lateral shuffles
Single-leg squats and Nordic hamstring curls

fitness_components.name: Aerobic Endurance
fitness_components.description: Base cardio for 90 minutes. Build with long runs (30-45 min) and tempo runs.

fitness_components.name: Sprint Speed
fitness_components.description: Short bursts of 10-30m. Train with hill sprints, sled pulls, and flying sprints.

fitness_components.name: Agility & COD
fitness_components.description: Change of direction speed. Ladder drills, T-test, and reactive agility.

fitness_components.name: Strength & Power
fitness_components.description: Leg strength for shooting, jumping, tackling. Squats, lunges, plyometrics.

fitness_components.name: Flexibility & Mobility
fitness_components.description: Prevent injury with dynamic warm-up and post-training stretching. Hip flexor and hamstring focus.

fitness_components.name: Recovery
fitness_components.description: Sleep, nutrition, hydration, and active recovery. Critical in Indian heat and humidity.

progression_path: Build base fitness with regular running (3x per week)
Add football-specific interval training
Incorporate agility and ladder work
Develop strength with bodyweight exercises
Progress to gym-based strength training
Peak for match days with periodization
common_mistakes: Skipping warm-up and cool-down
Only doing long-distance running (football is intermittent)
Not hydrating enough in Indian heat
Training too hard the day before a match
Ignoring hamstring and groin flexibility

skill_areas.name: Set Pieces (Free Kicks, Corners, Penalties, Throw-ins)
skill_areas.description: Free kick technique, corner delivery, penalty taking, throw-in routines, and rehearsed set piece plays. Games are won and lost on set pieces.
pain_points: Cannot bend free kicks over the wall
Inconsistent corner delivery
Missing penalties under pressure
No rehearsed set piece routines
Long throw-ins not utilized
Defensive set piece organization is poor
drills: Free kick curl and dip practice (20 reps daily)
Corner delivery to near post, far post, and penalty spot
Penalty practice under simulated pressure (sprint before taking)
Set piece routine rehearsal with team: short corners, dummy runs
Knuckleball technique from 25-30 yards
Long throw-in practice (for outfield players)

set_piece_types.name: Curled Free Kick
set_piece_types.description: Bend ball over wall into corner. Strike side of ball with inside of foot.

set_piece_types.name: Knuckleball Free Kick
set_piece_types.description: Hit dead center with no spin. Ball moves unpredictably in the air.

set_piece_types.name: Power Free Kick
set_piece_types.description: Blast through the wall with pace. Low trajectory, minimal spin.

set_piece_types.name: Dipping Free Kick
set_piece_types.description: Top-spin strike that rises over wall then dips sharply. Bale/CR7 technique.

set_piece_types.name: Corner (Inswinger)
set_piece_types.description: Corner curling toward goal. Creates chances with goalkeeper under pressure.

set_piece_types.name: Corner (Outswinger)
set_piece_types.description: Corner curling away from goal. Easier to attack with a running header.

set_piece_types.name: Penalty
set_piece_types.description: Pick your spot, commit, follow through. Mental composure is the key skill.

set_piece_types.name: Long Throw-in
set_piece_types.description: Treated like a set piece. Arc the ball into the box from the touchline.

progression_path: Master basic penalty technique and composure
Learn curled free kick technique
Develop consistent corner delivery
Add knuckleball and dipping free kicks
Design and rehearse team set piece routines
Study opposition for set piece weaknesses
common_mistakes: Changing mind during penalty run-up
Not enough practice reps on free kicks
Hitting free kicks with too much pace and not enough technique
Poor run-up angle on free kicks
Defensive team not organized for opposition corners

skill_areas.name: First Touch & Ball Control
skill_areas.description: Receiving the ball cleanly with any surface: feet, thigh, chest. A great first touch creates time and space. The difference between good and great players.
pain_points: Ball bouncing off when receiving a hard pass
First touch too heavy, losing possession
Cannot control aerial passes (chest/thigh)
Touch always in front instead of to the side
No awareness of surroundings before receiving
drills: Wall passing: receive and control with inside/outside foot
Aerial ball control: throw up, chest down, play
Partner drill: receive under pressure from behind
Rebounder net: first touch into space
Juggling for ball feel: feet, thighs, head

touch_types.name: Inside Foot Cushion
touch_types.description: Soft touch with inside of foot. Withdraw foot on contact to absorb pace.

touch_types.name: Outside Foot Touch
touch_types.description: Control with outside of foot. Opens body to play forward quickly.

touch_types.name: Sole Control
touch_types.description: Trap ball under sole. Quick stop for tight spaces. Futsal fundamental.

touch_types.name: Thigh Control
touch_types.description: Cushion aerial balls with thigh. Relax muscle on contact.

touch_types.name: Chest Control
touch_types.description: Use chest to bring high balls down. Arch back to cushion, or push chest forward to redirect.

touch_types.name: Directional First Touch
touch_types.description: Touch ball into space away from pressure in one movement. Scan before receiving.

progression_path: Practice basic inside foot receiving against a wall
Add outside foot and sole control
Develop aerial control: chest and thigh
Practice receiving under passive pressure
Master directional first touch with scanning
Apply in match situations at speed
common_mistakes: Stiff ankle instead of relaxed cushioning
Not scanning before the ball arrives
First touch always toward pressure instead of away
Standing still to receive instead of moving to the ball
Not using the back foot to receive and turn

skill_areas.name: Heading
skill_areas.description: Attacking and defensive heading technique. Scoring from crosses, clearing from corners, and winning aerial duels.
pain_points: Closing eyes when heading
No power or direction on headers
Poor timing on jumps for aerial balls
Fear of heading the ball
Losing aerial duels to smaller opponents
drills: Standing header into goal from short crosses
Jumping header practice with served crosses
Defensive heading clearance from corners
Heading accuracy: aim for target zones
1v1 aerial duel practice
progression_path: Get comfortable heading a soft ball from close range
Practice standing headers with gentle serves
Add jumping headers with timing
Develop defensive clearing headers
Win aerial duels under physical challenge
common_mistakes: Closing eyes on contact (most common issue)
Heading with top of head instead of forehead
Not jumping to meet the ball at highest point
Heading backwards instead of generating forward power
Not using arms for balance and elevation

skill_areas.name: Weak Foot Development
skill_areas.description: Systematic development of the non-dominant foot for passing, shooting, and dribbling. Being two-footed doubles your effectiveness.
pain_points: Avoiding weak foot completely in matches
No power or accuracy with weak foot
Defenders forcing onto weak foot and winning easily
Weak foot touch is heavy and inaccurate
Cannot cross or shoot with weak foot
drills: Weak foot wall passing: 100 reps daily
Weak foot shooting: 50 shots at goal daily
Dribble with weak foot only through cones
Weak foot juggling: target 50 consecutive touches
Play small-sided games using only weak foot
progression_path: Start with simple weak foot passing against a wall
Add weak foot shooting from close range
Dribble with weak foot in training
Use weak foot in non-pressured match situations
Gradually increase weak foot usage to 30-40% of touches
Become truly two-footed: no defender can force you one way
common_mistakes: Expecting instant results (takes months of daily work)
Only practicing when convenient instead of daily
Not committing to using weak foot in matches
Practicing only one skill (e.g., only passing, not shooting)
Giving up after a few weeks

skill_areas.name: Tactical Awareness & Game Intelligence
skill_areas.description: Understanding formations, pressing triggers, positional play, and reading the game. Football IQ separates average from elite players.
pain_points: Not knowing when to press and when to hold
Poor positioning in formation
Cannot read opposition patterns
Always reactive instead of anticipating
Does not understand role in different formations

formations.description: Classic formation. Two banks of four with striker partnership. Solid defensively, direct in attack.
Wide wingers and single pivot. Great for pressing and quick transitions. Most popular modern formation.
Three centre-backs with wing-backs providing width. Midfield overload. Popular in ISL.
Double pivot protection with creative CAM. Balanced between defence and attack.
Aggressive attacking shape. Requires very fit wing-backs and disciplined pressing.

tactical_concepts.name: Pressing Triggers
tactical_concepts.description: When to press: bad touch, backwards pass, goalkeeper receiving. Recognizing moments to win the ball high.

tactical_concepts.name: Counter-Attack
tactical_concepts.description: Quick transitions from defence to attack. Exploit space behind high defensive lines.

tactical_concepts.name: Positional Play
tactical_concepts.description: Occupy correct spaces to create passing options. Width, depth, and spacing.

tactical_concepts.name: Off-the-Ball Movement
tactical_concepts.description: Runs behind, check-to-feet, overlaps, underlaps. Creating space for yourself and teammates.

tactical_concepts.name: Defensive Shape
tactical_concepts.description: Maintaining compact shape as a unit. Sliding across the pitch together.

tactical_concepts.name: Build-Up Play
tactical_concepts.description: Playing out from the back through midfield. Patient possession to find openings.

progression_path: Learn your role in one formation
Understand basic pressing triggers
Study off-the-ball movement patterns
Watch tactical analysis videos (Tifo Football, The Athletic)
Learn multiple formations and your role in each
Develop ability to read the game and anticipate
common_mistakes: Pressing alone without team coordination
Leaving position to chase the ball
Not scanning before receiving
Standing still when your team has the ball
Not adjusting position when the ball moves

skill_areas.name: Off-the-Ball Movement & Positioning
skill_areas.description: Intelligent runs, finding space, creating passing lanes, and making yourself available. You only have the ball 2-3 minutes in a 90-minute game - the rest is off-the-ball.
pain_points: Standing still waiting for the ball
Making runs that are never seen
Not checking shoulders before receiving
Running into offside positions
Not creating space for teammates

movement_types.name: Run Behind
movement_types.description: Sprint into space behind the defensive line. Time run to stay onside.

movement_types.name: Check to Feet
movement_types.description: Drop toward the ball to receive to feet. Creates space behind you.

movement_types.name: Diagonal Run
movement_types.description: Angled run across defenders. Harder to track than straight runs.

movement_types.name: Overlap
movement_types.description: Full-back or wide midfielder runs outside the winger. Creates 2v1 on the wing.

movement_types.name: Underlap
movement_types.description: Inside run behind the winger. Creates overload centrally.

movement_types.name: Decoy Run
movement_types.description: Run to drag defenders away, creating space for a teammate. Selfless but crucial.

progression_path: Start making basic runs behind the defence
Learn to check to feet and play with back to goal
Add diagonal runs and movements off the shoulder
Understand when to overlap and underlap
Make decoy runs for teammates
Read the game to time runs perfectly
common_mistakes: Running before the passer is ready to play
Always running forward instead of sometimes checking short
Not timing the run to stay onside
Making the same run every time (becoming predictable)
Not communicating the run to the passer

skill_areas.name: Futsal & Indoor Football Skills
skill_areas.description: Futsal-specific techniques that also enhance outdoor football ability. Close control, sole usage, quick thinking, and small-space play. Futsal is booming in Indian cities.
pain_points: Not comfortable using the sole of the foot
Too slow in tight spaces
Cannot play one-touch futsal style
Dribbling too wide for indoor dimensions
Not adapting to the heavier, low-bounce futsal ball

futsal_techniques.name: Sole Roll
futsal_techniques.description: Control and move ball using sole of foot. The fundamental futsal touch.

futsal_techniques.name: Toe Poke Finish
futsal_techniques.description: Quick jabbed finish with toe. Faster release than laces in tight spaces.

futsal_techniques.name: Sole Drag Turn
futsal_techniques.description: Drag ball with sole while turning. Change direction quickly in tight spaces.

futsal_techniques.name: Pivot Play
futsal_techniques.description: Receive with back to goal, hold up, and lay off. Central futsal role.

futsal_techniques.name: 1-2 Combinations
futsal_techniques.description: Rapid give-and-go passing. The heart of futsal attacking play.

futsal_techniques.name: Power Play GK
futsal_techniques.description: Goalkeeper as outfield player. 5v4 overload in futsal when chasing the game.

progression_path: Get comfortable with sole rolls and sole control
Learn toe poke finishing
Practice 1-2 combinations at speed
Develop pivot play and hold-up
Apply futsal skills to outdoor 11-a-side football
common_mistakes: Playing futsal like outdoor football (too wide, too long)
Not using the sole enough
Standing still after passing instead of moving
Taking too many touches in tight spaces
Not adapting to the low-bounce futsal ball

skill_areas.name: Freestyle Tricks & Juggling
skill_areas.description: Freestyle football tricks, juggling, and showboating skills. Builds incredible ball feel and coordination. Great for fun and social media content.
pain_points: Cannot juggle more than 10 times
Ball goes everywhere when trying tricks
No confidence to try tricks in games
Cannot do around-the-world or rainbow flick

freestyle_tricks.name: Basic Juggling
freestyle_tricks.description: Keep ball in the air with feet, thighs, head. Build to 50+ consecutive.

freestyle_tricks.name: Around the World
freestyle_tricks.description: Circle foot around the ball while juggling. Classic freestyle fundamental.

freestyle_tricks.name: Rainbow Flick
freestyle_tricks.description: Roll ball up calf and flick over head. Showstopping in matches.

freestyle_tricks.name: Crossover
freestyle_tricks.description: Cross legs while juggling. Develops coordination and body control.

freestyle_tricks.name: Akka (Ground Moves)
freestyle_tricks.description: Ground-based freestyle tricks. Panna (nutmeg), akka 3000, and more.

progression_path: Master basic juggling to 50 consecutive
Learn around-the-world and stalls
Add rainbow flick and crossover
Combine tricks into sequences
Develop ground moves (akkas) for practical use
common_mistakes: Kicking the ball too high when juggling
Not relaxing the ankle
Trying advanced tricks before mastering basics
Practicing on uneven surfaces

striker.description: Strikers need clinical finishing, intelligent movement, and composure in the box. Target men need heading and hold-up play; speed merchants need pace and running behind.

winger.description: Wingers need 1v1 dribbling, crossing ability, pace, and the ability to cut inside and shoot. Must be two-footed and supremely fit for recovery runs.

CAM.description: The creative hub. Needs vision, passing range, first touch, and the ability to score from distance. Must link midfield to attack.

CM.description: Box-to-box midfielders need endurance, passing, and the ability to contribute at both ends. The engine room of the team.

CDM.description: The shield in front of defence. Needs positional intelligence, tackling, passing under pressure, and aerial ability. Controls the game's tempo.

centre_back.description: Must read the game, win aerial duels, and play out from the back in modern football. Communication and leadership are essential.

full_back.description: Modern full-backs are the most demanding position. Must defend, attack, overlap, cross, and have elite fitness. Inverted full-backs cut inside to play as extra midfielders.

goalkeeper.description: The last line of defence and first line of attack. Modern keepers must save shots, command the box, distribute with feet, and sweep behind the defence.
goalkeeper.role_models: Alisson (complete), Ederson (sweeper), Courtois (shot-stopper), Gurpreet Singh Sandhu (India)

weekly_plan.breakdown: 15 min ball mastery, 15 min passing, 10 min shooting/skill, 10 min fitness

beginner.description: New players should build ball comfort with both feet, basic passing accuracy, and base fitness. Focus on touches on the ball - aim for 500+ touches per training session.

weekly_plan.breakdown: 10 min warm-up, 20 min position-specific, 20 min match scenarios, 15 min fitness, 10 min cool-down

intermediate.description: Developing players should specialize in their position, understand tactics, and improve match-specific skills like shooting under pressure and defensive positioning.

weekly_plan.breakdown: 15 min activation, 25 min technical, 25 min tactical/team, 15 min set pieces, 10 min recovery

advanced.description: Competitive players should master set pieces, develop tactical intelligence, become truly two-footed, and train at match intensity. Study the game through video analysis.
